county_metadata.parent_county: Original (unorganized territory -- originally Davis County, renamed 1889)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Geary County formed 1855 as Davis County (renamed Geary 1889). County seat is Junction City. Named for John W. Geary, territorial governor. Fort Riley military reservation is within the county (established 1853). Military families have been a major population component since territorial days.
county_metadata.research_tips: Geary County (originally Davis County until 1889) Register of Deeds in Junction City holds land records from 1855. Fort Riley is within the county -- NARA holds extensive military records for the fort (established 1853). Junction City developed as a military support town. The Geary County Historical Society and Museum in Junction City has local collections. BLM GLO land patents. The multi-ethnic military community produced diverse church records. Probate Court records from 1855. Junction City Union newspaper at KSHS (one of the oldest Kansas papers). FamilySearch has microfilmed Geary County records. When searching early records, use 'Davis County' for the pre-1889 period.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Geary County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Junction City

source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival from 1855; deed books are substantially complete

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Gove County formed 1868, organized 1886. County seat is Gove City. Named for Captain Grenville L. Gove, killed in Civil War. Western Kansas agricultural county. Sparsely populated. Quinter Mennonite Brethren community. Monument Rocks (Chalk Pyramids) natural landmark.
county_metadata.research_tips: Gove County Register of Deeds in Gove City holds land records from the 1880s (practical settlement). A sparsely populated western Kansas county. The Gove County Historical Society has local family files. BLM GLO land patents are particularly important for tracing original homesteaders. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Church records from pioneer Catholic and Methodist congregations. Gove County newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Quinter was a significant Mennonite Brethren community; check church archives. Dust Bowl effects caused significant outmigration in the 1930s.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Gove County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Gove City

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Graham County formed 1867, organized 1880. County seat is Hill City. Named for Captain John L. Graham, killed in Civil War. Northwestern Kansas county. Nicodemus (in Graham County) is the only remaining western town established by African American Exodusters (1877) and is a National Historic Site.
county_metadata.research_tips: Graham County Register of Deeds in Hill City holds land records from the 1880s. The Graham County Historical Society has local collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Church records from pioneer Lutheran, Methodist, and Catholic congregations serving German-Russian and other settlers. Hill City newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. The county experienced Dust Bowl effects in the 1930s. Nicodemus National Historic Site (in neighboring Graham County area) documents one of the most significant Exoduster (African American) communities in Kansas -- KSHS and NPS hold related records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Graham County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Hill City

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Grant County formed 1873, organized 1888. County seat is Ulysses. Named for Ulysses S. Grant. Far-southwestern Kansas county. Late settlement. The original town of Ulysses was relocated in 1909. Dust Bowl effects were severe.
county_metadata.research_tips: Grant County Register of Deeds in Ulysses holds land records from the 1880s. A far-southwestern Kansas county with late settlement. The Grant County Museum in Ulysses has local collections. BLM GLO land patents are critical for this public-land county. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Church records from pioneer congregations. Ulysses newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. The original town of Ulysses was relocated in 1909. Dust Bowl effects were severe here. Natural gas and irrigated agriculture brought later families; check KSHS for agricultural records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Grant County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Ulysses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Gray County formed 1881, organized 1887. County seat is Cimarron. Named for Alfred Gray, Kansas Secretary of State. Santa Fe Trail crossing. County seat war between Cimarron and Ingalls in the 1880s. Cimarron gunfight (1889).
county_metadata.research_tips: Gray County Register of Deeds in Cimarron holds land records from 1887 (practical organization). Formed from Ford and Finney counties -- check predecessor records for earlier entries. The Gray County Historical Society has local collections. Cimarron was the site of a famous gunfight (1889) and the Gray County seat war. BLM GLO land patents. Probate Court records from 1887. Church records from pioneer Methodist and Catholic congregations. Cimarron and Ingalls newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. The Santa Fe Trail crossed through the county; check KSHS for trail-related records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Gray County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Cimarron

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Greeley County formed 1873, organized 1888. County seat is Tribune. Named for Horace Greeley, newspaper editor ('Go West, young man'). One of the most sparsely populated Kansas counties. Severe Dust Bowl effects. Far-western Kansas on the Colorado border.
county_metadata.research_tips: Greeley County Register of Deeds in Tribune holds land records from the 1880s. One of the most sparsely populated Kansas counties. The Greeley County Historical Society has local family files. BLM GLO land patents are essential for tracing homesteaders. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Very few church records from the earliest period due to sparse settlement. Tribune newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Dust Bowl devastation in the 1930s caused massive population loss. The county has one of the latest organization dates in Kansas.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Greeley County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Tribune

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Greenwood County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Eureka. Named for Alfred Burton Greenwood, Commissioner of Indian Affairs. Flint Hills ranching region. Fall River and Verdigris River valleys. Oil production from the early 1900s.
county_metadata.research_tips: Greenwood County Register of Deeds in Eureka holds land records from 1855. The county is in the Flint Hills with early cattle ranching records. The Greenwood County Historical Society and Museum in Eureka has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents. Early settlement along the Verdigris and Fall rivers. Church records from Methodist, Baptist, and Presbyterian congregations from the 1860s. Probate Court records from 1855. Eureka Herald newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Greenwood County records. The county produced oil in the early 1900s; petroleum-related records may supplement genealogical research.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Greenwood County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Eureka

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hamilton County formed 1873, organized 1886. County seat is Syracuse. Named for Alexander Hamilton. Far-western Kansas county along the Arkansas River. Santa Fe Trail corridor. Irrigation agriculture. Dust Bowl effects were severe.
county_metadata.research_tips: Hamilton County Register of Deeds in Syracuse holds land records from the 1880s. A far-western Kansas county along the Arkansas River. The Hamilton County Museum in Syracuse has local collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Church records from pioneer congregations are sparse. Syracuse newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. The county experienced severe Dust Bowl effects. Mexican American railroad workers settled here from the early 1900s; check Santa Fe Railroad records at KSHS. Irrigation agriculture brought later families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Hamilton County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Syracuse

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Harper County formed 1867, organized 1878. County seat is Anthony. Named for Sergeant Marion Harper, killed in Civil War. Borders Oklahoma (Cherokee Outlet). Anthony was a staging point for the 1893 Cherokee Outlet opening. Mennonite communities.
county_metadata.research_tips: Harper County Register of Deeds in Anthony holds land records from 1867. The county borders Oklahoma and was near the Cherokee Outlet. The Harper County Historical Society and Museum has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents. Anthony was a staging point for the 1893 Cherokee Outlet opening. Church records from Methodist, Baptist, and Mennonite congregations from the 1870s. Probate Court records from 1867. Anthony newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Harper County records. District Court naturalization records. The county had Mennonite communities; check Bethel College archives.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Harper County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Anthony

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Harvey County formed 1872 from McPherson and Sedgwick counties. County seat is Newton. Named for James M. Harvey, Kansas governor. Newton was a major Santa Fe Railroad division point and cattle shipping town. Bethel College (Mennonite) in North Newton is the center of Kansas Mennonite heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Harvey County Register of Deeds in Newton holds land records from 1872. Newton was a major cattle shipping point and Santa Fe Railroad division headquarters. The Harvey County Historical Society and Museum in Newton has excellent local collections. Bethel College in North Newton holds the Mennonite Library and Archives -- essential for tracing Mennonite families who settled heavily in Harvey County from the 1870s. German-Russian Mennonite church records are particularly rich. BLM GLO land patents. Probate Court records from 1872. Newton newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Harvey County records. The Kansas Mennonite Historical Committee records at Bethel College document migration patterns.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Harvey County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Newton

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records begin from practical settlement in the 1872s-1882s

source_profiles.source_name: Harvey County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1880-1950 for Harvey County; Kansas Territory census 1855 and 1859 may include Harvey County entries at KSHS; state census 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905, 1915, 1925 at KSHS

source_profiles.source_name: Harvey County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (homestead, preemption, timber culture, cash entry) for Harvey County; Kansas is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Harvey County Mennonite and German-Russian Church Records
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / Bethel College Mennonite Library and Archives, North Newton / KSHS
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mennonite church records (General Conference, Mennonite Brethren, Church of God in Christ Mennonite) including baptisms, marriages, burials, and membership rolls for Harvey County; Bethel College Mennonite Library and Archives holds church records, immigration documents, and passenger lists from Russia/Ukraine

source_profiles.source_name: Harvey County Marriage Records -- Probate Court / KSHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Harvey County Probate Court, Newton / Kansas State Historical Society, Topeka
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records for Harvey County from 1872; Probate Court issued marriage licenses in Kansas; KSHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Kansas marriages; state vital records registration began 1911
